[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2005-02-23
[TITLE]: Is This a 'Polar Vortex' in Chicago? | Snopes.com
[METADATA/INTRO]: Pictures of cars, boats, trees, and other landscape features encrusted with ice from severe cold weather began circulating on the Internet in late January 2005, accompanied by viewer-added comments such as "Now this is what I'd call really cold... brrrrrrrrr";...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Pictures of cars, boats, trees, and other landscape features encrusted with ice from severe cold weather began circulating on the Internet in late January 2005, accompanied by viewer-added comments such as "Now this is what I'd call really cold... brrrrrrrrr"; "Sure glad that it doesn't get this cold here"; "So, How cold was it?"; and "Next time you're complaining while scraping the ice off your windscreen, think again!": 
 These photos are all over on Facebook. They are supposed to be on Lake Shore Drive in Chicago. I live in a condo building on Lake Shore Drive! I think these photos are fake or enhanced in some way. No way it is in the city limits! If it is real, it has to be somewhere way outside the city!!

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2016
[TITLE]: Thrillingly Freezing: Car Frozen Solid by Lake Erie in the U.S. - People's Daily Online
[METADATA/INTRO]: Although it has been reportedly a warm winter, some places in the world are nonetheless seeing the winter in its full force. Incredible photos show that a car was frozen solid after being parked by Lake Erie in Buffalo, New...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Although it has been reportedly a warm winter, some places in the world are nonetheless seeing the winter in its full force. Incredible photos show that a car was frozen solid after being parked by Lake Erie in Buffalo, New York in the U.S. on January 10, 2016. 
 Due to an arctic blast since Sunday night, the winds of 47 mph blew up huge waves on the lake, causing water spills on the car, until it was nailed frozen.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: Unknown Date
[TITLE]: See New York cars encased in ice after lake spray freezes vehicles to ground | Fox Weather
[METADATA/INTRO]: See New York cars encased in ice after lake spray freezes vehicles to ground Tropical storm-force winds, Lake Erie waves and cold temperatures encased this car in ice....
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: See New York cars encased in ice after lake spray freezes vehicles to ground 
 Tropical storm-force winds, Lake Erie waves and cold temperatures encased this car in ice. 
 HAMBURG, N.Y. – No, this is not a wooly mammoth, it's an SUV in a very unfortunate parking spot. The driver parked lakeside in Hamburg, New York, and came back to their car frozen from lake spray. 
 This car probably had a beautiful view of Lake Erie on St. Patrick’s Day with temperatures above freezing. The temperature started dropping, and the wind picked up after that. On Saturday, winds gusted to 47 mph, which churned a healthy chop on the lake and a lot of sea spray.
